# Alkanes | Bitcoin Universe Protocol Reference

> Human docs: index.html
> Field reference: reference.html
> Network: Bitcoin
> Classification: Bitcoin smart contracts and DeFi

## Summary

A WebAssembly smart contract metaprotocol built on Protorunes, with execution and state indexed from Bitcoin transactions.

## State model

Rust-authored WASM contracts, deployed through Ordinals and interpreted by the Alkanes and Metashrew stack.

## Lifecycle

1. Write Rust
2. Compile WASM
3. Deploy Alkane
4. Call contract

## Operations

- Deploy: Publishes a contract binary and establishes an Alkane identity.
- Call: Invokes a contract method with protocol-defined inputs and transaction context.
- Index: Metashrew processes blocks and exposes contract state to apps and APIs.
- Compose: Contracts can consume and produce protocol assets within their declared logic.

## Safety

1. An Alkane call is not interchangeable with a generic token transfer. Review the contract source and method semantics first.
2. WASM correctness, indexer behavior, and wallet transaction construction all matter. Test on a non-production environment where available.
3. Treat contract IDs, ABI assumptions, and API responses as versioned integration inputs.

## Sources

- Alkanes documentation: https://alkanes.build/en/docs
- Alkanes source: https://github.com/sandshrewmetaprotocols/alkanes
